description: Logic behaves unreliably because a signal crosses between voltage domains without a level shifter.
body: Failure mode: a low-voltage output drives a high-voltage input directly, so the receiving gate cannot reliably distinguish a logic high, producing marginal or wrong levels. Detection signals: a level-shifter rule violation between domains, intermittent logic errors at the crossing, and behavior sensitive to the voltage difference. Do not close timing while a voltage crossing lacks a shifter. Safer fallback: insert the appropriate level shifter on the crossing, declare it in the power-intent file, and re-check the multi-voltage rules.
